howItWorks.intro: This body fat percentage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Navy Method (Male)
rules.explanation: US Navy body fat estimation from circumference measurements.

howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is a healthy body fat percentage?
faq.answer: Men: 10-20%, Women: 18-28%. Athletes are typically lower.

faq.question: How accurate is this method?
faq.answer: Navy method is ±3% compared to DEXA scans. Best for tracking trends over time.

faq.question: Is the Body Fat Percentage Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard fitness and training formulas trusted by athletes and fitness enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What sex should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate sex value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Body Fat Percentage Calculator?
sections.body: The Body Fat Percentage Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your sex, waist circumference, neck circumference, you get instant results including body fat percentage, category. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting body fat percentage right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your sex and need to find the right body fat percentage. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Sex
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your sex — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: waist circumference, neck circumference, height, hip circumference. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your body fat percentage, category. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your sex before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both body fat percentage and category — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your hip circumference, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
